front.title: Requirements and Gaps for Post-Quantum Certificate Rotation in Multi-Tenant Public Key Infrastructure Environments

abstract.t: Organizations operating Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) across multiple isolated tenant environments face a critical gap: existing PKI management protocols and standards do not address the coordination requirements for post-quantum cryptographic (PQC) algorithm migration in shared, multi-tenant certificate authority deployments. This document identifies the functional requirements and open protocol gaps that must be addressed to enable safe, consistent, and auditable PQC certificate rotation across multi- tenant PKI environments. No new protocol mechanisms are specified; this is an informational requirements document intended to motivate future standards work.
